Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Failure of the Thermodynamics of Computation(2010) 26 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 121 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 129 upvotes, 17 comments
  4. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 432 upvotes, 74 comments
  5. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1241 upvotes, 241 comments
  6. CSS is DOOMed 430 upvotes, 102 comments
  7. The Hackers Who Tracked My Sleep Cycle 36 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. Say No to Palantir in Europe 108 upvotes, 21 comments
  9. Linux is an interpreter 226 upvotes, 55 comments
  10. I decompiled the White House's new app 584 upvotes, 212 comments
  11. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 172 upvotes, 62 comments
  12. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 37 upvotes, 9 comments
  13. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 110 upvotes, 40 comments
  14. Overestimation of microplastics potentially caused by scientists' gloves 328 upvotes, 144 comments
  15. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 174 upvotes, 75 comments
  16. A laser-based process that enables adhesive-free paper packaging 111 upvotes, 46 comments
  17. Show HN: Public transit systems as data – lines, stations, railcars, and history 35 upvotes, 11 comments
  18. Lat.md: Agent Lattice: a knowledge graph for your codebase, written in Markdown 71 upvotes, 31 comments
  19. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 124 upvotes, 66 comments
  20. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 67 upvotes, 32 comments
  21. Siclair Microvision (1977) 37 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. The Epistemology of Microphysics 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 166 upvotes, 103 comments
  24. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 238 upvotes, 159 comments
  25. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 184 upvotes, 121 comments
  26. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 219 upvotes, 152 comments
  27. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 715 upvotes, 569 comments
  28. Nonfiction Publishing, Under Threat, Is More Important 35 upvotes, 22 comments
  29. Building a Mostly IPv6 Only Home Network 35 upvotes, 32 comments
  30. Android’s new sideload settings will carry over to new devices 131 upvotes, 187 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
